name: schemapack

metadata.name: schemapack
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: Make your JSON Schemas sociable and create linked data model.

documentation:
![tests](https://github.com/ghga-de/schemapack/actions/workflows/unit_and_int_tests.yaml/badge.svg)
[![PyPI version shields.io](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/schemapack.svg)](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/schemapack/)
[![PyPI pyversions](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/schemapack.svg)](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/schemapack/)
[![Coverage Status](https://coveralls.io/repos/github/ghga-de/schemapack/badge.svg?branch=main)](https://coveralls.io/github/ghga-de/schemapack?branch=main)

# schemapack

Make your JSON Schemas sociable and create linked data model.

## Installation
This package is available at PyPI:
https://pypi.org/project/schemapack

You can install it from there using:
```
pip install schemapack
```

## Development
For setting up the development environment, we rely on the
[devcontainer feature](https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/remote/containers) of vscode.

To use it, you have to have Docker as well as vscode with its "Remote - Containers"
extension (`ms-vscode-remote.remote-containers`) extension installed.
Then, you just have to open this repo in vscode and run the command
`Remote-Containers: Reopen in Container` from the vscode "Command Palette".

This will give you a full-fledged, pre-configured development environment including:
- infrastructural dependencies of the service (databases, etc.)
- all relevant vscode extensions pre-installed
- pre-configured linting and auto-formating
- a pre-configured debugger
- automatic license-header insertion

Moreover, inside the devcontainer, there is follwing convenience command available
(please type it in the integrated terminal of vscode):
- `dev_install` - install the lib with all development dependencies and pre-commit hooks
(please run that if you are starting the devcontainer for the first time
or if added any python dependencies to the [`./setup.cfg`](./setup.cfg))

## License
This repository is free to use and modify according to the [Apache 2.0 License](./LICENSE).

functions.name: schemapack.main.load_and_validate
documentation:
Load and validate a datapack with respect to a schemapack using the default
validation plugins.

Raises:
    schemapack.exceptions.SchemaPackFormatError:
        If the schemapack has an invalid format.
    schemapack.exceptions.DataPackFormatError:
        If the datapack has an invalid format.
    schemapack.exceptions.ValidationError:
        If the datapack is not valid against the schemapack.

functions.name: schemapack.utils.read_json_or_yaml
documentation:
Read JSON or YAML data from file. The format is decided based on the file
extension. If the file extension is not recognized, YAML is assumed (as it is a
superset of JSON).

Raises:
    DecodeError: If the file cannot be decoded as JSON or YAML.

functions.name: schemapack.utils.assert_valid_json_schema
documentation:
Asserts that the given string is a valid JSON Schema.

Raises:
    JsonSchemaError: If the schema is invalid.

documentation: Change the current working directory temporarily within a with block.

functions.name: schemapack.isolate.identify_dependencies
documentation:
Identify all dependencies (recursively) for a given resource
of the given class in the given datapack. Please note that it is assumed that
the datapack has already been validated against the schemapack.

Args:
    datapack:
        Containing the target resource and all dependencies.
    class_name:
        The class of the target resource.
    resource_id:
        The id of the target resource.
    schemapack:
        The schemapack used for looking up the classes of relations.
    include_target:
        Set to true if the target dependency shall be included in the result.
    _resource_blacklist:
        A mapping containing resources (resource ids as values and class names as
        keys) that shall be ignored during the dependency resolution and thus will
        not appear in the result. This is only used internally for recursion.

Returns:
    A mapping containing resource ids (values) of dependencies by class names
    (keys).

Raises:
    KeyError:
        If the resource_class or the resource_id does not exist in the schemapack
        or datapack.
    schemapack.Exceptions.ValidationAssumptionError:
        If it became apparent that the datapack was not already validated against
        the schemapack.

functions.name: schemapack.isolate.downscope_datapack
documentation:
Downscope a datapack to only contain the given resources.

Args:
    ignore_non_existing:
        Controls how to handle resources from the resource_map that are not in the
        datapack. If set to `True`, these resources will be ignored. If set to
        `False` (default), a KeyError will be raised.

Raises:
    KeyError:
        If a resource from the resource_map is not in the datapack and
        ignore_non_existing is set to `False`.

functions.name: schemapack.isolate.isolate
documentation:
Isolate a resource from a non-rooted datapack to created a rooted datapack. I.e.
the resulting datapack will only contain resources referenced by the root resource
as well as the root resource itself.

Initiate a SpecError.

An error indicating that an instance was not valid against the specification.

An error indicating that the provided data is not valid against the schemapack

Initiate a SchemaPackSpecError.

Args:

An error indicating that the provided data is not valid against the datapack

documentation:
Initiate a ValidationPluginError.

Args:

Raised by a ValidationPlugin.

classes.name: schemapack.exceptions.ValidationErrorRecord
documentation:
A record of an Error occuring during validation on a specific context
(e.g. a specific resource) regarding a specific validation aspect.

Attributes:
    subject_class:
        If relevant, the name of the subject class as per the schemapack.
    subject_resource:
        If relevant, the ID or the subject resource of the subject class.
    type:
        A preferably short type label.
    message:
        A human-readable message describing the error.
    details:
        A dictionary for transporting additional machine-readable details.

classes.name: schemapack.exceptions.ValidationError

methods.name: schemapack.exceptions.ValidationError.__init__
documentation:
Initiate a ValidationError.

Args:
    records: A list of ValidationErrorRecords.

A collection of ValidationErrorRecords raised when a datapack fails validation
against a schemapack.

classes.name: schemapack.exceptions.ValidationAssumptionError

methods.name: schemapack.exceptions.ValidationAssumptionError.__init__
documentation:
Initiate a ValidationAssumptionError.

Args:
    context: Some context to be included in the error message.

This is raised when a unit of code assumes to work with a datapack that has
already been validated against a specific schemapack, however, it became apparent
that this assumption was not correct. The error should not contain the details on
which aspect is invalid. These details can be retrieved by performing a validation
and receiving a ValidationError.

classes.name: schemapack.exceptions.CircularRelationError
documentation:
Raised when a circular relation between resources is detected, but the requested
operation cannot be performed on datapacks with circular relations.

functions.name: schemapack.integrate.integrate
documentation:
Integrate a rooted datapack into a nested json object-compatible data structure.
It is assumed that the provided datapack has already been validated against the
provided schemapack.

Args:
    datapack:
        The datapack to be integrated. Must be rooted.
    schemapack:
        The schemapack to be used for looking up the classes of relations.
    _resource_blacklist:
        An optional blacklist of resources (a mapping with resource ids as values
        and class names as keys) that cause an error to be raised if they are
        encountered when assembling the json compatible object. This is only used
        internally to prevent circular relations.
    _alt_root_class_name:
        An optional alternative root class name to be used instead of the class
        name specified as root in the datapack. This is only used internally for
        recursive calls.
    _alt_root_resource_id:
        An optional alternative root resource id to be used instead of the resource
        id specified as root in the datapack. This is only used internally for
        recursive calls.

Raises:
    ValueError:
        If the datapack is not rooted.
    schemapack.exceptions.ValidationAssumptionError:
        If the datapack is not valid against the schemapack.
    schemapack.exceptions.CircularRelationError:
        If a circular relation is detected.

classes.name: schemapack.validation.base.GlobalValidationPlugin

methods.name: schemapack.validation.base.GlobalValidationPlugin.does_apply
documentation:
A classmethod to check whether this validation plugin is relevant for the
given schemapack.

Returns: True if this plugin is relevant for the given schemapack.

methods.name: schemapack.validation.base.GlobalValidationPlugin.__init__
documentation: This plugin is configured with the entire schemapack.

methods.name: schemapack.validation.base.GlobalValidationPlugin.validate
documentation:
Validate the entire datapack.

Raises:
    schemapack.exceptions.DataValidationError: If validation fails.

Abstract class for a plugin that performs validation of a datapack with respect
to the entire schemapack.

Please note:
A ValidationPlugin should always just check for one single aspect of validation
and should not perform all checks possible with a schemapack.
The "global" scope only means that objects implementing this protocol do not focus
on one specific datapack class or resource but rather on a validation
aspect that applies to the entire datapack.

classes.name: schemapack.validation.base.ClassValidationPlugin

methods.name: schemapack.validation.base.ClassValidationPlugin.does_apply
documentation:
A classmethod to check whether this validation plugin is relevant for the
given class definition.

Returns: True if this plugin is relevant for the given class definition.

methods.name: schemapack.validation.base.ClassValidationPlugin.__init__
documentation: This plugin is configured with one specific class definition of a schemapack.

methods.name: schemapack.validation.base.ClassValidationPlugin.validate
documentation:
Validate all resources of a specific class. The entire datapack is provided
for resolving relations to other classes.

Raises:
    schemapack.exceptions.DataValidationError: If validation fails.

Abstract class for defining class-scoped validation plugin. Instances
of implemeting concrete classes perform validation with respect to one
specific schemapack class.

classes.name: schemapack.validation.base.ResourceValidationPlugin

methods.name: schemapack.validation.base.ResourceValidationPlugin.does_apply
documentation:
A classmethod to check whether this validation plugin is relevant for the
given class definition.

Returns: True if this plugin is relevant for the given class definition.

methods.name: schemapack.validation.base.ResourceValidationPlugin.__init__
documentation: This plugin is configured with one specific class definition of a schemapack.

methods.name: schemapack.validation.base.ResourceValidationPlugin.validate
documentation:
Validates a specific resource of the defined class. The entire datapack is
provided for resolving relations to resources of other classes.

Raises:
    schemapack.exceptions.DataValidationError: If validation fails.

Abstract class for defining resource-scoped validation plugin. Implementing
objects perform validation with respect to one specific resource of a schemapack
class.

Validate all resources of a specific class. The entire datapack is provided

A resource-scoped validation plugin validating that no overlap in one_to_many
relations exists across resources of a schemapack class.
This only applies to schemapack classes with one_to_many relations.

classes.name: schemapack.spec.schemapack.Cardinality
documentation:
Cardinality of a relation.
Read as `{this class}_to_{foreign class}`. I.e. MANY_TO_ONE means that this class
may have many instances that point to the same foreign class instance.

classes.name: schemapack.spec.schemapack.RelationLookupMethod
documentation:
The method used to lookup the foreign class instance(s) referenced in a
relation.

classes.name: schemapack.spec.schemapack.FrozenBaseModel
documentation: A BaseModel that cannot be changed after initialization.

classes.name: schemapack.spec.schemapack.IdField
documentation: A model for describing a schemapack ID field definition.

classes.name: schemapack.spec.schemapack.ContentSchema

methods.name: schemapack.spec.schemapack.ContentSchema.json_schema_dict
documentation: A dict representation of the JSON schema.
cannot be cached because pydantic includes cached properties in the hash and this
is unhashable:

methods.name: schemapack.spec.schemapack.ContentSchema.properties
documentation:
Returns a set of the content properties. If the content schema is not a
JSON Schema Object (but e.g. a list), an empty set is returned.

methods.name: schemapack.spec.schemapack.ContentSchema.validate_schema
documentation: Make sure that the schema is a valid JSON Schema.
A model for describing a schemapack content schema.

classes.name: schemapack.spec.schemapack.Relation
documentation: A model for describing a schemapack relation definition.

classes.name: schemapack.spec.schemapack.ClassDefinition

methods.name: schemapack.spec.schemapack.ClassDefinition.content_schema_validator
documentation:
Validate and convert the type of the content schema.

If a str is provided, it must be a path to the JSON schema file and not the
JSON-formatted string representation of the JSON schema itself.

methods.name: schemapack.spec.schemapack.ClassDefinition.content_schema_serializer
documentation:
Serialize the content schema by representing it either as the path to the
JSON schema (if the path is known) or as the JSON schema itself.

methods.name: schemapack.spec.schemapack.ClassDefinition.relation_name_validator
documentation: Validate relation names.

methods.name: schemapack.spec.schemapack.ClassDefinition.id_from_content_validator
documentation:
Validate that the from_content property of the id field is part of the
content schema.
A model for describing a schemapack class definition.

classes.name: schemapack.spec.schemapack.SchemaPack

methods.name: schemapack.spec.schemapack.SchemaPack.check_schemapack_field_exists
documentation:
Checks that the schemapack field exists and points to a supported version
before doing anything else. Please note, this validation only takes place if the
data is passed as dict. However, the data can be pretty much anything.

methods.name: schemapack.spec.schemapack.SchemaPack.class_name_validator
documentation: Validates class names.

methods.name: schemapack.spec.schemapack.SchemaPack.relation_to_class_validation
documentation: Validate that all relations point to existing classes.
A model for describing a schemapack definition.

classes.name: schemapack.validation.main.SchemaPackValidator

methods.name: schemapack.validation.main.SchemaPackValidator.__init__
documentation:
Initialize with a specific schemapack.

Args:
    schemapack:
        The schemapack to validate against.
    add_global_plugins:
        Global validation plugins to use in addition to the default ones.
    add_class_plugins:
        Class validation plugins to use in addition to the default ones.
    add_resource_plugins:
        Resource validation plugins to use in addition to the default ones.

methods.name: schemapack.validation.main.SchemaPackValidator.validate
documentation:
Validates the given datapack against the configured schemapack. The execution
order of validation plugins is as follows:
(1) Run global validation plugins
(2) Only upon success, execute class validation plugins.
(3) Only upon success, run resource validation plugins.
The reason for this conditional execution is that class plugins are assuming a
intact state on the global level. Similarly, resource plugins are assuming an
intact state on the class level.

Raises:
    schemapack.exceptions.ValidationError: If any validation issues are found.
A class for validating arbitrary datapacks against a specific schemapack.
